primary_concern: Sepsis with multi-organ dysfunction and AKI
clinical_summary: Patient demonstrates sepsis criteria per Sepsis-3 definitions with lactate 4.2 mmol/L (>2 mmol/L), WBC 14.2 K/uL (>12 K/uL), and evidence of acute kidney injury (AKI Stage 3 creatinine 8.9 mg/dL [excluded outlier] with prior Stage 1). Coagulopathy is present with platelets persistently <100 K/uL (88-95 K/uL). Elevated bilirubin (1.4 mg/dL) and anemia (Hgb 9.8 g/dL) suggest possible hepatic involvement or hemolysis.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and AKI meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection plus SOFA increase ≥2 points
Creatinine rise >0.3 mg/dL in 48h with Stage 3 classification based on excluded outlier value (8.9 mg/dL)
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or ≥1.5× baseline

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coag score ≥3 with bleeding risk
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but no documentation of vasopressor-dependent hypotension
prioritized_risks.threshold: Vasopressor-dependent MAP ≥65 mmHg + lactate >2 mmol/L post-fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per local sepsis protocol
Order blood cultures and infection-focused workup
Start vasopressors if hypotension persists despite fluids
Monitor platelet trends and consider transfusion if <50 K/uL
Assess for AKI reversal strategies (fluid optimization, nephrotoxin review)
doctor_handoff: Review excluded creatinine outlier (8.9 mg/dL) for specimen integrity. Confirm source of infection and assess for need for ICU admission. Evaluate for renal replacement therapy eligibility given Stage 3 AKI.

disease_progression.observation: Initial sepsis markers: WBC 14.2 K/uL, Hgb 9.8 g/dL, Bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
